title: Claims Operations Intervention
decision: decide whether to replicate the intervention in two additional regions
prompt:
Create a seven-slide case study for operations executives deciding whether to replicate a claims-processing intervention. Show the initial state, intervention, adoption path, measured before/after outcomes, operator evidence, limitations, and replication decision. Use the supplied data exactly. Include a before/after comparison, editable process diagram, outcome chart, and evidence panel. Do not imply causality beyond the observed before/after association.

Design direction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

attachments.content: The intervention introduced structured intake, one review checkpoint, and exception routing. The benchmark authorizes an observed before/after association only; it does not establish a causal effect.

designDirection.label: Evidence editorial
designDirection.instruction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

trace.summary: The user consented to send the full brief and 2 uploaded data sources to OpenRouter. The named Claude Sonnet 5 model supplied the narrative plan through pi-ai; NodeSlide normalized, persisted, and validated the deck deterministically. Self-critique: 2 passes: revised to fix visual_composition_repeat visual-rhythm issue (1 -> 0 issues).
